title: Skere Kaan
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Kaan}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Lord_Kaan_EGF.jpg]] |name=Skere Kaan |homeworld=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |birth= |death=[[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] ('''[[Great ReSynchronization|965BrS]]'''), [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]In ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' Kaan's hair color is described as white, whereas in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'' and ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' it is dark brown. |feathers= |eyes=*Blue *[[Color/Legends|Red]] {{C|[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]}} |skin=Tan |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] {{C|formerly}} *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] {{C|formerly}} *[[New Sith]] Empire *[[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] |masters= |apprentices=[[Vulta Daanat]]''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'' }} {{Quote|A man of vision.|[[Githany]]|}} '''Skere Kaan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the last years of the [[New Sith Wars]] before defecting to become a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and creating the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] within the [[New Sith|reorganized Sith Empire]], earning him the nickname of the '''"Dark One."''' Ruling the Sith for a decade, Kaan's reign was turbulent as the Brotherhood was rife with civil unrest, and conflict with the Jedi saw his numbers dwindle. A few Sith were starting to question his beliefs, including [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]. At the end of the bloody [[Ruusan campaign]], Kaan, his followers, and much of the Jedi [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] were destroyed by a [[thought bomb]] of his own creation during the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]. In Kaan's place, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], who deceived the entire Brotherhood into its destruction, set up a [[Order of the Sith Lords|new Sith order]] with the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]]: one [[Sith Master/Legends|master]] and one [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|This victory sends a message to the Republic and the Jedi. Now they will truly know and fear the Brotherhood.|Skere Kaan|}} A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] born during the [[Republic Dark Age]], Skere Kaan was born a commoner before being discovered by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. Sophisticated and knowledgeable of economic policy and fleet command, Kaan became a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and a beacon for hope among members of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] who hoped to finally destroy the [[New Sith|reformed Sith Empire]] which had been [[New Sith Wars|plaguing]] the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] for nearly a millennium. Having perfected the art of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], Jedi Kaan was considered a valuable asset by the High Council, despite his radical belief that the Republic and the Jedi were responsible for the anarchy which plagued [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In an effort to moderate Kaan's extremist views, the High Council awarded him the title of Jedi Master. Seeing the High Council as overbearing, Kaan led a group of loyal Knights away from Coruscant and formed the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] in [[1010 BBY]]. Declaring himself [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]],{{GamerCite|5|Darkness Shared}} his supporters referred to him as the "Dark One."{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} [[File:BrotherhoodOfDarkness-BoS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kaan surrounded by his Sith acolytes]] At the time of Kaan's ascent, the Sith Empire had fractured, with dozens of Sith Lords claiming the title of Dark Lord and waging endless campaigns against each other, rather than uniting to bring down the ailing Republic. Sensing that their endless quest for supremacy and power would destroy the Sith, Kaan set about reuniting the various splinter states under his banner,''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' destroying all those who refused to bend the knee until he was in control of the Sith Empire. The two most powerful Sith warlords—[[Kopecz]] and [[Qordis]]—pledged themselves to his cause, and they were followed by others, such as [[Kas'im]], [[LaTor]]''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' and [[Kaox Krul]]. Still unwilling to see what was growing to become an even worse threat, the Jedi High Council issued congratulatory thanks to Kaan for killing the worst of the Sith warlords during his campaign to amass power. However, Kaan had not lulled all Jedi into a state of ignorance; indeed, [[Jedi Lord]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]] saw Kaan for the threat he was and left Coruscant to gather his own forces. His idea of cooperation and equality among the Sith Lords was unique in their history. Rather than challenge each of the self-proclaimed "Dark Lords" and threaten the war effort, Kaan acquiesced to their vanity, and named them all "Dark Lord of the Sith," and declared "All are equal in the Brotherhood of Darkness." But he forbade any Sith to take the historic title of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]], since he held that title as being solely responsible for the jealousy and infighting that had destroyed the Sith in the past. With his charisma and power, the Sith struck back at the Republic and the Jedi Order. However, the Republic and the Order were not without their defenders. Having seen Kaan for what he truly was, Lord Hoth of the Jedi Order rallied the banners of the [[Jedi Grand Council]] and formed the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] through the support of all standing Jedi Lords. Once amassed, Hoth led his armies on a hunt for Sith, a decade-long war that would see great casualties on both sides. Although the conflict was widely regarded as an all-out war, the Republic claimed that it was simply fighting a series of engagements against an illegal organization. Despite Hoth's best efforts, a startling early victory at the [[Battle of Korriban (New Sith Wars)|Battle of Korriban]] enabled the Sith to retake their [[Korriban/Legends|ancestral homeworld]]. Kaan authorized the reopening of the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] there, and placed [[Qordis]] in charge to begin training the next generation of warriors. The reinvigorated Sith pushed the Jedi back, winning great victories against the Army of Light. Kaan proved to be a brilliant strategist, and an excellent tactician, capable of winning victory after victory against the Jedi forces, all the while putting his charisma to good use by luring many Jedi to serve him. While Kaan was a charismatic and skilled general, his rule was more fragile than it seemed, a weakness that would be exploited as the war ground on. As long as the victories continued, however, the Sith would remain united behind him. ===Nearing the end=== {{Quote|Deploying air forces… flanking armies… you are thinking like a dirt general, not a Sith Lord!|[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]|}} [[File:Kaan.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Kaan prepares to destroy the Jedi.]] Kaan tried to provoke Hoth into a confrontation, laying waste to worlds such as Bespin, Sullust, and Taanab, but his main strategy seemed to have already been to push towards the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], with Sith armies seizing [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] as a staging-ground in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], and winning battles on [[Trandosha/Legends|Trandosha]] and [[Phaseera]]. Kaan's next target was [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], a world near Kashyyyk, mostly insignificant except for its crucial role as a staging ground for attacks on Sith-held Kashyyyk. Striking without warning, the Sith fleet staged a devastating assault on the unprepared planet, completely overwhelming the planet's meager defenses. From his flagship [[Nightfall (Sith Destroyer)|''Nightfall'']], Kaan directed the campaign, utilizing his skill in [[Battle meditation/Legends|Battle meditation]] to confound the Republic defenders and bolster his own forces. The [[First Battle of Ruusan]] was an absolute victory for the Sith. Republic forces were repulsed again from Ruusan in the [[Second Battle of Ruusan]], and Kaan launched an ambitious invasion of the Core with his fleet, thrusting into the [[Bormea sector/Legends|Bormea sector]] to conquer [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]], and [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]. The Sith seemed almost poised to conquer [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and the Jedi seemed not to be attempting to defend the Core. However, Lord Hoth had gathered a Jedi army, known as the Army of Light, and had arrived on Ruusan to defeat the remaining Sith forces in the [[Third Battle of Ruusan]], and wipe out the Brotherhood, once and for all. By the [[Fourth Battle of Ruusan]], the war had moved to the surface of the planet itself, and, though outnumbered and out-manned by the Army of Light, Kaan sensed victory—the Jedi had spread themselves far too thin in a vain attempt to protect the inhabitants of Ruusan. In addition, the defection of the Jedi [[Githany]] gave the Sith vital intelligence on the Jedi battle plan. Though the Sith were suffering high casualties, Kaan believed he would prevail, and sent for reinforcements. While most of his followers remained loyal, a few prominent Dark Lords within the Brotherhood began to question his leadership: Githany, Kopecz, and most notably, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]. With the attentions of the Sith Lords focused against the Jedi on Ruusan, the tide of the war seemed to be turning against the Sith, with the Republic reclaiming areas like the [[Stenness Node]]. Bane was opposed to Kaan's battle tactics against the Jedi, believing that Lord Kaan was acting more like an ordinary military commander than a Dark Lord of the Sith, and was not properly using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Lord Kaan tried to kill Bane—first, by sending Kas'im to murder him, then, when that failed, by having Githany poison him. Bane was too strong—and survived both assassination attempts. Bane returned and declared Kaan unworthy of his title, but not before sending to Kaan a peace offering—a scroll inscribed with an ancient Sith ritual—the thought bomb. While surprised at Bane's survival, Lord Kaan accepted Darth Bane's tactical advice. Using the Force and attacking as one, the members of the Brotherhood meditated and focused their dark energy into Bane. The combined power of the Sith [[Sixth Battle of Ruusan|devastated]] the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]], and victory was in the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] grasp. Even as the Jedi defeat was imminent, Lord Kaan and the other members of the brotherhood broke the meditation circle. Impatient and overconfident, Kaan believed that the Jedi could be quickly and easily defeated if the Sith took to the field. Secretly, Kaan and his followers were jealous of Bane and his power, and felt that Darth Bane was using their energy for his own benefit. ===The thought bomb=== {{Quote|I ask that you join me in one last task: the creation of a weapon so powerful that when it is detonated, the victors shall become the vanquished and be swept from the pages of history.|Kaan|}} [[File:Kaan-crazy.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kaan, driven mad by the dark side, prepares to use the [[thought bomb]].]] When the Sith Masters broke their meditation and took the field, however, the Jedi counterattacked, and the Sith took severe losses. This was caused in part by Darth Bane's machinations, having signaled the Sith armada in orbit to break formation and attack the opposing Republic fleet, allowing Jedi reinforcements to land. Most of the Sith Masters died in the battle. Following one last discussion with Bane in his tent, Kaan took his defeated forces into a deep cave to wait for the Jedi's final [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|strike]], with the Brotherhood being in near defeat. There he used his ultimate tactic: a "[[thought bomb]]." Bane had provided the instructions to "a thought bomb" to Kaan, and successfully deceived him into believing that he and the rest of the Brotherhood would survive the effects of a thought bomb. Kaan believed that the thought bomb would destroy the Jedi, but was unaware that a thought bomb would engulf every [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force user]] within the radius due to Bane intentionally leaving that part out. Bane's deception would lead to not just Kaan's own doom, but that of every Force-user within the radius of the explosion. With the aid of LaTor, Kaan studied and refined the thought bomb ritual until he and the other Dark Lords were ready to unleash it. With Qordis dead, and Githany and Darth Bane having left the Brotherhood, it was left to Kopecz to defend against the approaching Jedi forces under [[Valenthyne Farfalla]]. When Lord Hoth and his retinue finally arrived and confronted the Sith, Lord Kaan detonated the thought bomb, killing himself, his Sith followers and Lord Hoth's Jedi. The New Sith Wars were over and the Sith presumed extinct. The caves where Kaan and his Brotherhood had died would eventually be known as the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], his apprentice [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], and her cousin [[Darovit]] were those that survived the bomb, being distant from its effects and suffering little damage. Bane's callous assessment of his rival and former leader was that Kaan's "suicide" was his "smartest decision."''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' Kaan's soul remained trapped along with the other souls for over a thousand years until a Jedi named [[Kyle Katarn]] traveled to the Valley of the Jedi and freed them.[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] ===Legacy=== {{Quote|The forces who favor anarchy over structure have won. For what is this 'democracy' they speak of if not the absence of order? Of reason? Surely the strong should rule—for that is nature's way.|Kaan|}} Although the souls of all the Jedi and the Sith killed by the thought bomb were trapped in the Valley of the Jedi, what appeared to be the ghost of Kaan appeared to Bane after the battle, along with what appeared to be the ghost of Qordis, who had died before the detonation of the thought bomb. Bane concluded that the ghosts were hallucinations resulting from the thought bomb damaging his brain. Bane continued to see the ghosts of Kaan and Qordis until he used the power of his [[Orbalisk armor]] to unleash a burst of energy at them, reassuring himself that he didn't need to fear the spirits of Kaan or Qordis. [[Darth Millennial]] considered Kaan's philosophy of "Rule by the Strong" superior to Bane's "Rule of Two," leading him to leave his master, [[Darth Cognus]], and found the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]].{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} Centuries later, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], one of the last Dark Lords of Bane's order, used one of Kaan's indestructible [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulets]] in the construction of one of his own [[Glove of Darth Vader|gloves]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Kaan is a fool, Githany.''"
"''You've never even met Lord Kaan. I have. He's a great man, Bane. A man of vision.''"
"''He's as blind as an Orkellian cave slug.|[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] and [[Githany]]|}} [[File:Kaan-flyer.jpg|thumb|right|Lord Kaan on his flier]] Unlike many Sith Lords, Kaan was handsome, charming and charismatic, claiming that all were equal in the Brotherhood of Darkness. Nevertheless he was manipulative, frequently using [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]] to ensure the loyalty of his followers. He chose those who were weak willed to ensure their loyalty, though certain members of his Brotherhood were strong willed enough to resist him, notably Bane and Kopecz. Bane regarded Kaan as both a coward and a fool and believed that he did not deserve the title of Dark Lord of the Sith. Ironically, despite his mastery of mind tricks, Kaan himself was weak willed and preached Rule by the Strong to hide his own weakness. It was this weakness that allowed his defeats in the last days of his life to drive him insane, which eventually led to him being deceived by Bane into unintentionally causing his death and the destruction of the Brotherhood. During the [[Ruusan campaign]] Kaan had a cavernous hideout in the valley that would be later known as [[Valley of the Jedi]], that was safe from bomb and missile attacks. Large chambers had been dug out to make the place suitable for him and his men. He had decorated his room with a throne made of [[bone/Legends|bones]]; from the throne radiated two lines of stands composed of the standing embalmed bodies of dead leaders and their banners. ==Powers and abilities== Kaan was exceptionally skilled in the use of [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]], which he used in an attempt to ensure the loyalty of his followers. He bent the minds of his followers, so his army remained pliant. He used this power also to pull the other Sith out of the ritual Bane led to rain lightning in a massive storm that almost lasted long enough to wipe out the Army of Light. Certain members of the Brotherhood of Darkness were able to resist him, notably Bane and Kopecz, though Kopecz had to use more effort than Bane. When he put out [[Force call|his call to all the Sith]], even Bane could not refuse to follow the summons to Russan forever. In addition to his mastery of mind tricks, Kaan was a master of battle meditation who could use it to change the course of wars, which he used both in fleet and land war to guide his allies and bring despair to those who he fought unless they were covered by a Jedi using the same power; He also could [[Force sense/Legends|sense]] space battles around himself while meditating and [[Precognition/Legends|sometimes even knew how ships would act before they did]]. He was a skilled military strategist, though he was fixated too much on the Force and spiritual themes of war, as opposed to just leaving the Jedi and winning with ships and logistics,''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' the Sith was a skilled rider of fliers[[Jedi vs. Sith 4|''Jedi vs. Sith 4'']] and a powerful warrior with a grip like durasteel. When Lord Hoth forced him to either give up, die, or use his last and most dread weapon, the Sith was quick enough to trigger his thought bomb just before the Jedi Lord could take his life. Although Darth Bane thought he was a fool, when asked by his newly acquired apprentice, Darth Zannah, if he was weak, he thought to himself that Kaan was many things, but weak was not one of them. In the hours before his "surrender", Kaan was full of hatred; Force energy caused the air to simmer near him, like heat, [[Ionize|caused static to nearby devices]] and headache to beings standing near him as his eyes glowed and will [[Hatred|painfully beat]] at those around him. Sometimes he had a purple glow, born of his deep attunement to the dark side. His last and most infamous act, was his leading his followers in the use of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|a Sith ritual]] to create a [[thought bomb]], which he detonated, dragging his army and the Jedi who came to force an end to the war into a millennium of torment. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Lord Kaan's first name is a tip of the cap to Shere Khan, the Tiger King in the Jungle Book. The two characters share a penchant for suave, beard-stroking villainy and seductive manipulation of their followers.|[[Paul R. Urquhart|Paul Urquhart]]|{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/21728447315/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-4|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 4|date=2012-04-24}}}} Kaan's birth date is unknown, but author [[John Jackson Miller]] briefly joked that Kaan would be in "high school" during the events of [[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']].{{WebCite|url=http://www.2bitgeek.com/article/knight-errant-panel-with-john-jackson-miller-celebration-v.html|text=Knight Errant panel with John Jackson Miller|archivedate=20250404181910}} If this is true, Kaan would have been born around 1,045 BBY. Skere Kaan's name was first revealed in the [[2012]] ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''. It was chosen as a reference to [[Wikipedia:Shere Khan|Shere Khan]], the tiger antagonist from [[Wikipedia:Rudyard Kipling|Rudyard Kipling's]] [[Wikipedia:The Jungle Book|''The Jungle Book'']].{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/Jason_Fry_Facebook_QA_Recap_144784.asp|text=Jason Fry Facebook Q&A Recap|date=2012-04-11|archivedate=20190829210801}} ==Appearances== [[1010 BBY]]–[[1006 BBY]] |before=[[Belia Darzu]] |before-years= |after=Himself with [[Qordis]], [[Kopecz]], [[Kas'im]], et al. |after-years=[[1006 BBY]]–[[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] }} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] with [[Qordis]], [[Kopecz]], [[Kas'im]], et al. |years=[[1006 BBY]]–[[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] |before=Himself, alone |before-years=[[1010 BBY]]–[[1006 BBY]] |after=[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] with [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] |after-years= }} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |bg=Кан |de=Skere Kaan |es=Skere Kaan |it=Skere Kaan |pl=Skere Kaan |pt=Legends:Skere Kaan }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kaan, Skere}} [[Category:Coruscanti]] [[Category:Dark Lords of the Sith of the New Sith Wars]] [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Force ghosts]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Members of the Brotherhood of Darkness]] [[Category:Pre-Ruusan Jedi Masters]] [[Category:Sith Masters]]
